message.content:
POLARIS MISSION (this wheel): Make the agentic outline top-benchmark: it must COMPUTE-and-PROVE numbers (the moat frontier products lack), process the FULL 346-basket corpus with cp4_used=agentic (never fallback-plain; seed-fallback only on GLM-5.2 reasoning truncation), and build the rich toolkit + hard battery from the redesign doc so the downstream report scores top on RACE/DeepTRACE. Read /workspace/POLARIS/docs/agentic_outline_redesign.md (read-only) for the binding T1/T2 spec.
SUCCESS IS MEASURED BY: (a) A computed number (e.g. a delta/ratio) can be COMPUTED by the agent AND RENDER in the outline via the verified [#calc:] lane (quantified_analysis.py:617), never via [CITE:ev_xxx] — proven end-to-end on one real case; (b) the T1 toolkit tools land and pass real unit tests; (c) the T2 battery runs and S0/S1 failures trend to zero. Faithfulness invariant is the hard gate: NO derived/unverified number may ever reach the [CITE:ev_xxx] render path (that would be a P0 breach).
The bar is not "bug fixed" — it is the benchmark number at the top, VERIFIED. Keep going until the
measurement says top (or you hit a hard external blocker you must report honestly).

FENCE (military discipline — no exceptions):
- Work ONLY inside /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent (branch bot/outline-agent-box). NEVER touch another worktree.
- NEVER edit anything under /home/polaris/polaris_project (operator's monitoring dir — deck.sh, wheel*.js, wheels.py).
  If you catch yourself editing a monitoring script or a workflow .js, you have DRIFTED — stop.
- Report REAL output only. Never invent, never claim a pass you did not observe, never fake a benchmark number.
- Commit every landed step in /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent; append one dated line to /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/WHEEL_PROGRESS.md.


YOU ARE FABLE — the scalpel. You are NOT here to read or search the codebase; Opus already did the
hard reading. You are here for ONE precise deep-think: judge what Opus did and decide the next step.

WHAT OPUS DID THIS ROUND:
{
  "did": [
    "Built the deterministic EMISSION channel end-to-end so the verified [#calc:] claim SENTENCE (not just the registry) reaches the FULL-CORPUS composer without any LLM hash-copy: (1) verified_compute tool records the render-ready sentence on new OutlineWorkspace.computed_claims {section, sentence, calc_token, input_ev_ids} + a `section` param; (2) _build_calc_claims_map at the seam (outline_agent.py) groups sentences by target section title, auto-homes unnamed claims by ev_id overlap with the final outline, drops unhomeable ones (never invents a section) -> new OutlineParseResult.calc_claims field; (3) generate_multi_section_report captures _outline_calc_claims (None => byte-identical) and threads it into _run_section; (4) _run_section._append_calc_claims deterministically appends the section's calc sentence(s) onto the strict_verify-bound draft at BOTH the primary (:6165) and regen (:6304) sites, idempotent via calc-token dedup.",
    "Proved the LIVE differential Fable flagged: new battery case h01g_emission_channel on the REAL Adobe delta with a TOKEN-FREE writer stub (as a real LLM would produce) — emission ON renders $590,507,000.00 in section verified_text via [#calc:]; emission OFF => number ABSENT (pre-fix live behavior); emission ON + no backing registry => appended token DROPPED (fail-closed, emission never bypasses strict_verify).",
    "Added a full-chain test (test_FULL_CHAIN_tool_to_map_to_composer_renders_number): agent tool call -> _build_calc_claims_map -> _run_section renders the number, proving no key-shape mismatch between seam output and composer input.",
    "Added 3 seam-export unit tests (explicit-section homing, auto-home by ev_id overlap, unhomeable-dropped) + exposed `section` in the verified_compute ToolDefinition.",
    "Confirmed byte-identical legacy: calc_claims defaults None everywhere; _append_calc_claims early-returns on empty; h01f (which omits calc_claims) still passes."
  ],
  "commits": [
    "9fc1c26"
  ],
  "files": [
    "src/polaris_graph/outline/outline_agent.py",
    "src/polaris_graph/outline/outline_toolkit.py",
    "src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py",
    "tests/polaris_graph/outline/test_moat_live_seam.py",
    "tests/polaris_graph/outline/test_outline_toolkit.py",
    "tests/battery/cases/calc_lane.py",
    "WHEEL_PROGRESS.md"
  ],
  "exercised": "Ran the real T2 battery (scripts/outline_battery.py --all): 9/9 PASS incl. new h01g_emission_channel driving the REAL _run_section composer on the Adobe FinanceBench delta with the LLM writer stubbed to emit token-free prose. Ran pytest over tests/polaris_graph/outline/ (63 passed) incl. the new emission, seam-export, and full-chain tool->map->composer tests. Verified module imports and confirmed no API keys in env.",
  "measurement": "Metric (a) CLOSED on the live-shaped composer path: battery h01g_emission_channel PASS — with a token-free writer draft, the derived $590,507,000.00 RENDERS in _run_section.verified_text via [#calc:opinc_delta:HASH:delta] only when the emission channel is ON (sentences_verified>=1), is ABSENT when OFF, and is DROPPED when the backing registry is absent. Metric (b): outline suite 63/63 green (was 56; +7 tests). Metric (c): T2 battery 9/9 PASS, S0=0 S1=0 S2=0 S3=0 S4=0 (was 8/8). Faithfulness gate intact (h01b + h01g-F drop guards). Numbers obtained by real runs: `pytest tests/polaris_graph/outline/` and `python scripts/outline_battery.py --all` (run 20260711T203201Z).",
  "open": [
    "OPERATIONAL (hard external blocker): no LLM API keys in env, so the 346-basket cp4_used=agentic RACE/DeepTRACE number cannot be produced this round — metric (a) is proven end-to-end on the exact composer path but not yet on a live full-corpus sweep.",
    "The deterministic append places the calc sentence AFTER the writer's prose rather than woven into a narrative sentence — RACE/DeepTRACE narrative-coherence impact of an appended (vs integrated) sentence is unmeasured until keys exist.",
    "corroboration_profile tool + decide-prompt CORE/index scaling still deferred."
  ],
  "status": "PROGRESS",
  "blockers": [
    "No LLM API keys (OPENAI/ANTHROPIC/GLM/ZHIPU/OPENROUTER all unset) — blocks the live 346-basket cp4_used=agentic RACE/DeepTRACE measurement."
  ]
}

OPUS'S PRECISE QUESTION FOR YOU:
The CODE gap you flagged is now closed: the verified [#calc:] sentence reaches the composer via a deterministic APPEND (after the writer's prose, before strict_verify), proven on the token-free-writer path (h01g). Judgment call for gap-to-top: is appending the calc sentence as a standalone trailing sentence acceptable for RACE/DeepTRACE narrative scoring, or does the number need to be WOVEN into a writer sentence — which would reintroduce the exact hash-copy problem (an LLM can't reproduce the spec_hash) and thus require a post-verify token-splice instead? If append-standalone is acceptable, then only credentials remain for metric (a) full-corpus; if not, that is a second CODE task I should build before a live sweep.

PRIOR ROUNDS (for continuity):
[
  {
    "r": 1,
    "meas": "Outline test suite went 19/19 -> 46/46 green (~2.0s). Metric (a) PROVEN with real numbers: Adobe FY2015->FY2016 operating-income delta $590,507,000.00 (real FinanceBench figures 1,493,602 - 903,095 thousands, *1000) COMPUTES via build_quantified_spec->execute_quantified_model and RENDERS via [#calc:opinc_delta:HASH:delta] -> strict_verify CALC LANE kept=1 dropped=0; the SAME derived number via [#ev:] -> kept=0 dropped=1 (number_not_in_any_cited_span). Metric (b): 7 T1 tools land + 21 real unit tests pass; verified_compute drives through the real _execute dispatch (rendered $1,493,602,000.00 via [#calc:m:HASH:v], model registered). Metric (c) T2 battery: NOT started this round (deferred), so S0/S1 not yet measurable.",
    "verdict": "CONTINUE",
    "next": "Build the T2 battery (scripts/outline_battery.py + cases/fixtures per the redesign doc) as the measurement instrument for metric (c), and include: (i) S0 calc-lane cases including a wrong-pair-subtraction semantic case (DEFER the mirror-model question<->formula entailment check — fail-open LLM checks cannot strengthen a fail-closed gate; add it only if this battery case shows the mode fires); (ii) one integration case that drives an outline-emitted [#calc:] sentence + workspace.quantified_models through the DOWNSTREAM strict_verify handoff — this will expose and force the fix that multi_section_generator.py:7955 and honest_pipeline.py:336 never receive the registry (plumb workspace.quantified_models into those calls so the computed number actually ships in production, not just in the probe); (iii) H07/H22 contradiction cases marked xfail until find_contradictions lands. Build fetch_url immediately after (trivial now via fold_in_fetched_rows at _fold_in.py:150), then find_contradictions/corroboration_profile to flip H07/H22 from xfail to measured."
  },
  {
    "r": 2,
    "meas": "T2 battery (run 20260711T200013Z, git a135789): 7/7 PASS, S0=0 S1=0 S2=0 S3=0 S4=0 (obtained via `python scripts/outline_battery.py --all`, recorded in outputs/battery/history.jsonl). Production handoff proven through the REAL run_honest_pipeline: legacy (quantified_models=None) => sentences_verified=0 and $590,507,000.00 ABSENT from final_report_text; with the registry => sentences_verified=1 and the number PRESENT in the production report. Outline unit suite: 54/54 green (~2s). Toolkit now 9 tools, each with real unit tests.",
    "verdict": "CONTINUE",
    "next": "Wire the registry across the live seam: (1) export workspace.quantified_models from run_outline_agent_or_legacy (outline_agent.py:2040) — cleanest is an extra attribute on parse_result or a widened return consumed at multi_section_generator.py:9669; (2) thread it (default None => byte-identical) into the section-body verify call sites — the verify_sentence_provenance calls at multi_section_generator.py:4437, 4701, 5442, 5886 and the strict_verify tail — so a [#calc:] body sentence verifies against the outline agent's computed models; (3) give the compose/writer path the verified claim sentences (verified_compute's claim text) as candidate body sentences so the token can actually be emitted; then prove it with a battery case that drives generate_multi_section_report (not run_honest_pipeline) from outline-agent workspace to number-in-report, and only after that attempt the full-corpus cp4_used=agentic run."
  },
  {
    "r": 3,
    "meas": "Metric (a) PROVEN on the full-corpus composer path (deterministic, offline): _run_section with the agentic registry renders \\\"Adobe's operating income rose by $590,507,000.00.[1][2]\\\" (sentences_verified=1, dropped=0; [1][2] resolve to the two 10-K input spans ev_2016/ev_2015); WITHOUT the registry the SAME body sentence is dropped and the section renders its gap stub (verified=0, number absent) — observed via a direct print of result.verified_text both ways. Metric (c): T2 battery 8/8 PASS, S0=S1=S2=S3=S4=0 (added h01f_composer_handoff). Outline suite 56/56 green. Regression: generator batch identical to HEAD baseline (11 pre-existing pollution failures, +0 from this diff, verified by HEAD swap). Faithfulness invariant intact: h01b drop-guard + calc_mixed_with_ev_token gate untouched, so derived numbers still cannot reach [#ev:]/[CITE:].",
    "verdict": "CONTINUE",
    "next": "Build the deterministic EMISSION channel (round-2 item 3, still open): export the render-ready claim sentences alongside the registry at the seam (outline_agent.py:2042 — e.g. parse_result.calc_claims: section_title -> [claim.render_sentence(...)], sourced from the agent's verified_compute results in final_ws), capture them next to _outline_quantified_models in generate_multi_section_report (:9705), and inject them DETERMINISTICALLY into the section's rewritten text immediately before the strict_verify call in _run_section (multi_section_generator.py:6160) — deterministic append, not prompt instruction, because an LLM cannot be trusted to copy a hash verbatim, and fail-closed safety is free: the appended sentence only survives if the registry verifies it (drop-guard h01b already proves the [#ev:] path stays sealed). Then re-prove the differential with a _call_section stub that does NOT pre-contain the token, add a battery case for it, and only then is the remaining gap purely credentials."
  }
]

DECIDE, precisely:
1. at_top: does the REAL measurement show top benchmark performance? ((a) A computed number (e.g. a delta/ratio) can be COMPUTED by the agent AND RENDER in the outline via the verified [#calc:] lane (quantified_analysis.py:617), never via [CITE:ev_xxx] — proven end-to-end on one real case; (b) the T1 toolkit tools land and pass real unit tests; (c) the T2 battery runs and S0/S1 failures trend to zero. Faithfulness invariant is the hard gate: NO derived/unverified number may ever reach the [CITE:ev_xxx] render path (that would be a P0 breach).)
2. correct: is the diff correct for what it claims?
3. faithfulness_safe: are the invariants intact (no derived/unverified number can render; fold-in seam intact)?
4. verdict: SIGN_OFF only if at_top AND correct AND faithfulness_safe. Else CONTINUE (or FIX_REGRESSION if
   Opus broke something). NEVER force-approve. A measurement Opus could not take is NOT a sign-off.
5. next_step: the single most important next action for Opus — precise, file:line where you can.
Think deeply here; this is the one place the wheel spends deep reasoning. Keep it tight and decisive.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
5724:def _append_calc_claims(
6203:    rewritten = _append_calc_claims(rewritten, section, calc_claims)
6359:        rewritten2 = _append_calc_claims(rewritten2, section, calc_claims)
9677:    _outline_calc_claims: dict | None = None
9773:        _outline_calc_claims = dict(getattr(outline_parse, "calc_claims", None) or {}) or None
9779:                len(_outline_quantified_models), len(_outline_calc_claims or {}),
10700:                calc_claims=_outline_calc_claims,
2063:    parse_result.calc_claims = _build_calc_claims_map(final_ws)
2068:def _build_calc_claims_map(final_ws: "OutlineWorkspace") -> dict[str, list[str]]:

def _append_calc_claims(
    rewritten: str, section: "SectionPlan", calc_claims: "dict[str, list[str]] | None",
) -> str:
    """MOAT DETERMINISTIC EMISSION: append the outline agent's verified [#calc:] claim sentence(s)
    for THIS section onto the strict_verify-bound draft.

    An LLM writer cannot be trusted to copy an unguessable ``spec_hash`` verbatim, so the
    render-ready sentence (produced by the agent's ``verified_compute`` tool and carrying its
    ``[#calc:model:hash:field]`` token) is appended DETERMINISTICALLY here — NOT via a prompt
    instruction. Fail-closed safety is free: the appended sentence survives the strict_verify below
    ONLY if the threaded ``quantified_models`` registry verifies its token (verify_modeled_atom
    re-checks the rendered digits against the registered re-execution); an unbacked/spoofed token is
    dropped ``no_provenance_token``, and a derived number STILL cannot reach the [#ev:] path.
    Empty/absent ``calc_claims`` (the legacy default) => byte-identical (returns ``rewritten``).
    Idempotent: a token already present in the draft is not re-appended (so the regen pass and any
    LLM emission of the same token never double-render)."""
    if not calc_claims:
        return rewritten
    sentences = calc_claims.get(getattr(section, "title", "")) or []
    if not sentences:
        return rewritten
    present_tokens = set(_CALC_TOKEN_RE.findall(rewritten or ""))
    out = (rewritten or "").rstrip()
    for raw_sentence in sentences:
        sentence = str(raw_sentence or "").strip()
        if not sentence:
            continue
        toks = set(_CALC_TOKEN_RE.findall(sentence))
        if toks and toks & present_tokens:
            continue  # already in the draft — do not duplicate the computed number
        present_tokens |= toks
        out = (out + " " + sentence).strip() if out else sentence
    return out


async def _run_section(
    section: SectionPlan,
    evidence_pool: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
    *,
    model: str,
    temperature: float,
    max_tokens_per_section: int,
    min_kept_fraction: float,
    contradictions: list[dict[str, Any]] | None = None,
    cross_trial_block: Any = None,  # CrossTrialSynthesisBlock | None
    use_field_agnostic_prompt: bool = False,
    advisory_text: str = "",  # I-meta-005 Phase 6 (#990): domain advisory append
    credibility_analysis: Any = None,  # I-cred-008b (#1162): advisory per-claim disclosure; None => byte-identical
    research_question: str = "",  # I-arch-004 F21 (#1255): framing-only; "" => byte-identical
    quantified_models: "dict[tuple[str, str], Any] | None" = None,  # MOAT: agentic verified-compute registry; None => byte-identical
    calc_claims: "dict[str, list[str]] | None" = None,  # MOAT EMISSION: per-section [#calc:] sentences; None => byte-identical
) -> SectionResult:
    """Run one section: generate, rewrite, verify, optionally regenerate.

    V32 (M-71) addition: when `contradictions` is non-None, this
    function injects a SECTION-LOCAL hedging instruction block into
    the prompt for sections whose subject/predicate keywords match
    high-severity contradictions. Codex strategic review 2026-04-25:
    Qwen flags hedging_appropriateness because explicit contradictions
    live only in the appendix; M-71 routes them into the body prose.

    V33 (M-72) addition: when `cross_trial_block` is non-None, this
    function injects per-section CROSS-TRIAL SYNTHESIS suggestions
    derived from already-rendered contract slot payloads. Codex
    run-12 verdict: V31+V32 lifted slot quality but Narrative depth
    stayed LB because Efficacy + Mechanism were slot-stacked. M-72
    generates 1-2 connective inferences (dose-response, comparator

# MOAT LIVE-SEAM: export the run-scoped verified-compute registry so the FULL-CORPUS
    # ``generate_multi_section_report`` composer can gate a ``[#calc:]`` body sentence against the
    # models the agentic loop actually computed. EMPTY ({}) => the consumer threads None =>
    # byte-identical legacy verify (derived numbers still cannot reach the [#ev:] render path; the
    # calc lane is the ONLY route and it is fail-closed on an empty/absent registry).
    parse_result.quantified_models = dict(final_ws.quantified_models)

    # MOAT DETERMINISTIC EMISSION: export the render-ready [#calc:] claim sentences keyed by their
    # target section title, so the FULL-CORPUS composer can APPEND them into the matching section
    # body deterministically (immediately before strict_verify), rather than trusting the LLM
    # writer to copy an unguessable spec_hash verbatim. Records with no explicit section are
    # auto-homed by matching their input ev_ids against the final outline's per-section ev_ids.
    # EMPTY ({}) => the consumer threads None => byte-identical legacy (no deterministic append).
    parse_result.calc_claims = _build_calc_claims_map(final_ws)

    return parse_result, retry_attempted, final_ws.total_input_tokens, final_ws.total_output_tokens


def _build_calc_claims_map(final_ws: "OutlineWorkspace") -> dict[str, list[str]]:
    """Group the agent's verified-compute claim sentences by their TARGET section title.

    A record with an explicit ``section`` is homed there verbatim. A record with an empty
    ``section`` is AUTO-HOMED to the final-outline section whose ``ev_ids`` overlap most with the
    claim's ``input_ev_ids`` (the sourced datapoints) — so a number derived from a section's own
    evidence lands in that section even when the agent did not name it. A record that homes to no
    section (no title, no ev_id overlap) is DROPPED from the emission map (it stays available to
    the writer prose only); it can never render a phantom section.
    Dedup: identical (section, calc_token) records collapse so a claim is appended at most once.
    """
    # section title -> set of ev_ids (from the FINAL outline draft the agent produced)
    sec_ev: list[tuple[str, set[str]]] = []
    for p in final_ws.outline_draft:
        title = _plan_field(p, "title", "") or ""
        ev_ids = set(_plan_field(p, "ev_ids", []) or [])
        if title:
            sec_ev.append((title, ev_ids))
    valid_titles = {t for t, _ in sec_ev}

    out: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
    seen: set[tuple[str, str]] = set()
    for rec in (final_ws.computed_claims or []):
        if not isinstance(rec, dict):
            continue
        sentence = str(rec.get("sentence") or "").strip()
        token = str(rec.get("calc_token") or "").strip()
        if not sentence or not token:
            continue
        target = str(rec.get("section") or "").strip()
        if target not in valid_titles:
            # Auto-home by ev_id overlap with the sourced datapoints.
            claim_evs = set(rec.get("input_ev_ids") or [])
            best_title, best_overlap = "", 0
            for title, ev_ids in sec_ev:
                overlap = len(claim_evs & ev_ids)
                if overlap > best_overlap:
                    best_title, best_overlap = title, overlap
            target = best_title if best_overlap > 0 else ""
        if not target:
            continue  # unhomeable — never invent a section for it
        dedup_key = (target, token)
        if dedup_key in seen:
            continue
        seen.add(dedup_key)
        out.setdefault(target, []).append(sentence)
    return out

input.gap_to_top: Only the live 346-basket cp4_used=agentic RACE/DeepTRACE sweep remains, and it is credential-blocked (I independently verified: zero LLM API keys in env — only CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ACCESS_TOKEN present). Everything measurable offline is green and I reproduced it myself: pytest 63/63 (2.39s), battery 9/9 with S0..S4=0 (run 20260711T203432Z, git 9fc1c26), and the emission code at _append_calc_claims (multi_section_generator.py:5724) + _build_calc_claims_map (outline_agent.py:2068) is fail-closed exactly as claimed (append happens pre-strict_verify; unbacked tokens drop no_provenance_token; unhomeable claims drop rather than invent a section; None => byte-identical). JUDGMENT ON OPUS'S QUESTION: append-standalone is ACCEPTABLE — do NOT build a post-verify token-splice. DeepTRACE scores claim-citation faithfulness, where a standalone precisely-tokened computed sentence is a strength; RACE narrative coherence tolerates a closing quantitative sentence (analytic reports routinely end paragraphs with a computed figure). Splicing the number into writer prose would bind a verified number to an UNVERIFIED surrounding claim — direction/subject could mismatch, which is exactly the h01c/h01d S0 wrong-adjacency class the battery exists to kill — or would require new hybrid re-verification machinery. The agent-authored sentence keeps prose and number atomically bound to the verified spec; weaving is a net-negative trade. Optional zero-risk polish only (not gap-blocking, unmeasurable without keys): feed the claim sentence SANS token into the writer prompt as context so the prose sets up the appended figure. One nit, not a breach: calc_claims keys on section title, so duplicate outline titles would render the same verified sentence in both sections — harmless (both verified) but worth a uniqueness guard someday.
